scenario_summary: An admin attempts to install two stable-channel installs on the same host (for example a per-user installed stable and a per-machine installed stable). Both installs would resolve to state.per_user_configuration_root.stable, the same update marker under the stable binary root, the same recent-item namespace recent_items_stable, the same aureline-stable:// protocol-handler scheme, and the same file-association candidacy. Every isolation row in pair.forbidden_two_stables fails by construction because state_root_map.yaml pins owning_channels to one channel per row.

explanation_fields.user_facing_summary: Only one stable install per host is supported. To run multiple Aureline installs on this host, install one as stable and the other as preview, beta, lts, or portable_stable. Aureline refuses the second stable install with a typed return code rather than silently merging or overwriting state.
explanation_fields.release_notes_summary: Two installs of the same channel on one host is unsupported by construction. Side-by-side coexistence is admissible only across distinct channels (stable + preview, stable + beta, stable + lts, installed + portable, or the published three-channel matrix).
explanation_fields.support_summary: If a host shows two installs of the same channel, capture an install-topology record for each and route through the install_review_packet support family; the rejection is a state-root-map violation, not a runtime bug.
explanation_fields.promotion_summary: Refuse. The pair.forbidden_two_stables admission row exists so tooling names the rejection rather than infers it. No release lane may admit a card that would collide with another card on owning_channels.

notes: Reference fixture for the forbidden_two_of_same_channel admission class. Mirrors the state_root_map.yaml no_shared_durable_state_across_channels rule.
